timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Egypt is unified under Narmer
description: Upper and Lower Egypt are united under a single ruler, conventionally identified as Narmer or Menes.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Early Dynastic Period begins
description: The first dynasties consolidate royal authority, administration, and early state institutions.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Old Kingdom begins
description: The Old Kingdom opens a major era of centralized monarchy and monumental building.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Step Pyramid of Djoser is built
description: The Step Pyramid at Saqqara marks a major development in royal monument construction.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Construction of the Great Pyramid begins
description: Khufu's pyramid at Giza becomes the largest and most famous monument of ancient Egypt.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Old Kingdom collapses
description: Central authority weakens and Egypt enters the First Intermediate Period.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Mentuhotep II reunifies Egypt
description: The reunification of Egypt ushers in the Middle Kingdom.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Hyksos dominate northern Egypt
description: The Hyksos establish control in the Nile Delta during the Second Intermediate Period.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Ahmose I expels the Hyksos
description: The expulsion of the Hyksos begins the New Kingdom, Egypt's imperial age.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Hatshepsut becomes pharaoh
description: Hatshepsut rules as king and sponsors major building projects and trade expeditions.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Tutankhamun takes the throne
description: Tutankhamun's reign follows the Amarna period and later becomes famous through his tomb.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Ramesses II begins his reign
description: Ramesses II becomes one of ancient Egypt's most powerful and celebrated rulers.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Battle of Kadesh is fought
description: Egypt and the Hittites clash at Kadesh in one of the best documented battles of the ancient world.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Egypt and the Hittites conclude a peace treaty
description: The treaty after Kadesh becomes one of the earliest known international peace agreements.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: New Kingdom ends
description: Imperial Egypt declines and the Third Intermediate Period begins.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Saite period begins
description: Native rulers of the 26th dynasty restore a measure of Egyptian power and cultural revival.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Persia conquers Egypt
description: Cambyses II defeats Egypt and incorporates it into the Achaemenian Empire.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Alexander the Great enters Egypt
description: Alexander's conquest ends Persian rule and begins the Hellenistic era in Egypt.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Ptolemaic dynasty is established
description: Ptolemy I founds a Macedonian ruling house that governs Egypt for nearly three centuries.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Rome annexes Egypt
description: After the defeat of Cleopatra VII and Mark Antony, Egypt becomes a Roman province.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and imperial milestones in ancient Egyptian history from unification to the Arab conquest.
title: Arab forces complete the conquest of Egypt
description: The Arab conquest ends the ancient Mediterranean succession of rule in Egypt.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Roman monarchy is overthrown
description: Rome replaces kingship with republican magistracies, beginning the Roman Republic.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: First secession of the plebs
description: Plebeians withdraw from Rome in protest, leading to the creation of the tribunate.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Twelve Tables are issued
description: Rome's first written legal code is drafted and publicly displayed.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Gauls sack Rome
description: A Gallic force captures Rome, leaving a lasting mark on Roman memory and military policy.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Licinian-Sextian reforms pass
description: The reforms open the consulship to plebeians and reshape republican politics.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Lex Hortensia is enacted
description: Resolutions of the plebeian assembly gain the force of law for all Romans.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: First Punic War begins
description: Rome enters a long struggle with Carthage for power in the western Mediterranean.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Second Punic War begins
description: Hannibal's invasion of Italy produces one of Rome's gravest crises.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Rome wins the Battle of Zama
description: Scipio Africanus defeats Hannibal and secures Roman victory in the Second Punic War.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Carthage is destroyed
description: Rome destroys Carthage at the end of the Third Punic War and dominates the western Mediterranean.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Tiberius Gracchus launches agrarian reform
description: His tribunate intensifies social conflict and inaugurates a turbulent reform era.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Sulla marches on Rome
description: Sulla's seizure of the city breaks republican norms and deepens civil conflict.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Sulla becomes dictator
description: Sulla uses dictatorship and proscriptions to reorder the Roman state.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Spartacus revolt begins
description: A major slave uprising challenges Roman authority in Italy.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: First Triumvirate is formed
description: Caesar, Pompey, and Crassus build an informal political alliance to dominate Roman politics.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Caesar crosses the Rubicon
description: Caesar's advance into Italy triggers a new civil war.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Caesar wins at Pharsalus
description: Caesar defeats Pompey in the decisive battle of the civil war.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Julius Caesar is assassinated
description: Caesar is killed on the Ides of March by senators who claim to defend the republic.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Second Triumvirate is established
description: Octavian, Antony, and Lepidus receive legal authority to restore order and pursue enemies.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Octavian wins the Battle of Actium
description: Octavian's naval victory over Antony and Cleopatra makes him the dominant Roman leader.

timeline_description: Key constitutional, military, and political milestones in the history of the Roman Republic from its founding to its transformation into the Roman Empire.
title: Augustan settlement ends the republic
description: Octavian receives the title Augustus, inaugurating the Roman Empire.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Osman I founds the Ottoman polity
description: Osman I is traditionally associated with the founding of the Ottoman state in northwestern Anatolia.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Bursa is captured
description: Bursa becomes the first major Ottoman capital and an early center of expansion.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Ottomans secure Gallipoli
description: The seizure of Gallipoli gives the Ottomans a lasting foothold in Europe.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Battle of Kosovo is fought
description: The battle strengthens Ottoman influence in the Balkans.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Bayezid I is defeated at Ankara
description: Timur's victory interrupts Ottoman expansion and triggers an interregnum.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Mehmed I restores the empire
description: Mehmed I reunifies the Ottoman domains after the interregnum.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Constantinople falls to Mehmed II
description: The Ottoman conquest of Constantinople transforms the empire into a major imperial power.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Ottomans win the Battle of Chaldiran
description: Selim I defeats the Safavids and secures Ottoman eastern power.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Mamluk Sultanate is conquered
description: Ottoman control expands into Syria, Egypt, and the holy cities of Islam.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Suleyman I begins his reign
description: Suleyman's accession opens the empire's classic age of power and law.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Battle of Mohacs is won
description: The Ottoman victory destroys the medieval kingdom of Hungary.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: First Ottoman siege of Vienna fails
description: The failed siege marks the limit of rapid Ottoman expansion into central Europe.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Ottoman fleet is defeated at Lepanto
description: The Holy League's victory checks Ottoman naval dominance in the Mediterranean.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Second siege of Vienna fails
description: The defeat at Vienna becomes a turning point in Ottoman military retreat.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Treaty of Karlowitz is signed
description: The treaty forces major territorial concessions and confirms Ottoman losses in Europe.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Treaty of Kucuk Kaynarca is signed
description: Russia secures major advantages, highlighting Ottoman weakness.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Tanzimat reforms begin
description: The Gulhane Edict launches a major program of administrative and legal reform.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Reform Edict expands Tanzimat principles
description: The imperial reform decree promises broader legal equality for subjects.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: First Ottoman constitution is promulgated
description: The empire briefly enters a constitutional era under pressure for reform.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Young Turk Revolution restores the constitution
description: Constitutional government is revived and imperial politics are transformed.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Armistice of Mudros ends Ottoman participation in World War I
description: The armistice leaves the empire militarily defeated and subject to Allied occupation.

timeline_description: Major dynastic, military, administrative, and diplomatic turning points in the history of the Ottoman Empire.
title: Ottoman sultanate is abolished
description: The Grand National Assembly ends the sultanate, bringing the empire to a close.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Newcomen steam engine enters use
description: Thomas Newcomen's engine becomes an early practical source of steam power for pumping water.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Flying shuttle is introduced
description: John Kay's flying shuttle accelerates weaving and reshapes textile production.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Spinning jenny is invented
description: James Hargreaves's machine greatly increases yarn output in textile manufacturing.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Watt patents improvements to the steam engine
description: James Watt's design helps make steam power far more efficient and versatile.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Spinning mule is developed
description: Samuel Crompton combines earlier textile technologies into a powerful new spinning machine.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Power loom is patented
description: Edmund Cartwright's loom furthers mechanization in the textile industry.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Cotton gin is patented
description: Eli Whitney's cotton gin dramatically speeds cotton processing and expands raw cotton production.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Trevithick demonstrates a steam locomotive
description: A high-pressure steam locomotive shows the potential of rail transport.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Stockton and Darlington Railway opens
description: The railway becomes a landmark in the public use of steam-powered transport.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Liverpool and Manchester Railway opens
description: The line demonstrates the commercial viability of intercity steam railways.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Factory Act regulates child labor
description: Britain passes major legislation to limit child labor in textile factories.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Electric telegraph is patented
description: The telegraph revolutionizes long-distance communication during industrialization.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Sewing machine is patented
description: Mechanical sewing advances factory production in the garment industry.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Bessemer process is announced
description: Henry Bessemer's method lowers the cost of steel production.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Transatlantic telegraph cable succeeds
description: Reliable cable communication links Europe and North America much more rapidly.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Second Industrial Revolution gathers pace
description: A new phase of industrialization expands steel, chemicals, electricity, and large-scale industry.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Telephone is patented
description: Alexander Graham Bell's patent marks a major step in voice communication technology.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Practical incandescent lamp is demonstrated
description: Electric lighting becomes increasingly viable for homes, streets, and factories.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Benz patents a motorcar
description: The patent motorwagen becomes a milestone in the rise of automotive transport.

timeline_description: Major technological, economic, and social milestones in the Industrial Revolution from early steam power to mass production.
title: Ford introduces the moving assembly line
description: The moving assembly line transforms industrial productivity and mass manufacture.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Luther issues the Ninety-five Theses
description: Martin Luther's challenge to indulgence preaching becomes a defining opening moment of the Reformation.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Papal bull Exsurge Domine condemns Luther
description: Pope Leo X formally denounces Luther's teachings and threatens excommunication.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Diet of Worms condemns Luther
description: Luther refuses to recant before the imperial assembly and is placed under the ban of the empire.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Luther's New Testament translation appears
description: The German New Testament widens access to scripture and shapes vernacular religion.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: German Peasants' War begins
description: Social unrest intersects with Reformation-era grievances across the Holy Roman Empire.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Early Anabaptist movement takes shape
description: Adult baptism and radical reform movements spread in Swiss and German lands.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Protest at Speyer gives Protestants their name
description: Princes and cities oppose imperial measures against reform, popularizing the term Protestant.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Augsburg Confession is presented
description: The confession becomes the central statement of Lutheran belief.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Act of Supremacy makes Henry VIII head of the Church of England
description: England breaks with papal authority and creates a royal national church.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Calvin publishes the Institutes of the Christian Religion
description: John Calvin systematizes a major current of Protestant theology.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Calvin returns to Geneva
description: Geneva becomes a major center of Reformed Protestantism under Calvin's leadership.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Council of Trent opens
description: The Catholic Church begins its major reforming and doctrinal response to Protestantism.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Peace of Augsburg is signed
description: The settlement recognizes Lutheranism and grants rulers limited confessional choice within the empire.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Elizabethan Settlement is enacted
description: Elizabeth I establishes a durable Protestant framework for the English church.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: French Wars of Religion begin
description: Conflict between Catholics and Huguenots destabilizes France for decades.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: St. Bartholomew's Day Massacre occurs
description: Thousands of Huguenots are killed in Paris and across France.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Edict of Nantes is issued
description: Henry IV grants limited toleration and rights to French Protestants.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Thirty Years' War begins
description: A major European war grows out of confessional and political tensions in the empire.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Peace of Westphalia is signed
description: The treaties help end the Thirty Years' War and reshape confessional politics in Europe.

timeline_description: Major theological, political, and institutional milestones in the Protestant Reformation and its aftermath.
title: Edict of Nantes is revoked
description: Louis XIV ends legal toleration for French Protestants, prompting renewed persecution and exile.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Archduke Franz Ferdinand is assassinated
description: The assassination in Sarajevo triggers the July Crisis among the European powers.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Austria-Hungary declares war on Serbia
description: The regional crisis becomes a general European war.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Germany invades Belgium and Britain enters the war
description: The invasion of Belgium brings Britain into the conflict.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: First Battle of the Marne halts Germany
description: Allied forces stop the German advance toward Paris and the war hardens into trench warfare.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Second Battle of Ypres begins
description: Large-scale poison gas use shocks the Western Front.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Gallipoli campaign begins
description: Allied forces land on the Gallipoli Peninsula in an unsuccessful attempt to break the deadlock.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Lusitania is sunk
description: The sinking intensifies international outrage at German submarine warfare.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Battle of Verdun begins
description: Verdun becomes one of the longest and most costly battles of the war.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Battle of the Somme begins
description: The Somme becomes a symbol of industrialized warfare and mass casualties.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Germany resumes unrestricted submarine warfare
description: The decision contributes directly to American entry into the war.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: February Revolution topples the tsar
description: Revolution in Russia weakens one of the major Allied powers.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: United States declares war on Germany
description: American entry strengthens the Allied war effort materially and politically.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Bolsheviks seize power in Russia
description: The October Revolution accelerates Russia's withdrawal from the war.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Treaty of Brest-Litovsk is signed
description: Soviet Russia makes peace with the Central Powers and exits the war.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: German Spring Offensive begins
description: Germany launches a major gamble on the Western Front before U.S. strength fully arrives.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Second Battle of the Marne turns the tide
description: The failed German attack is followed by a sustained Allied counteroffensive.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Hundred Days Offensive begins
description: A series of Allied victories breaks German resistance on the Western Front.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Armistice ends the fighting
description: Germany agrees to an armistice that ends combat on the Western Front.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Paris Peace Conference opens
description: Victorious powers meet to redesign the postwar international order.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: Treaty of Versailles is signed
description: The treaty imposes terms on Germany and helps shape the unstable postwar settlement.

timeline_description: Major military, political, and diplomatic milestones of World War I and its immediate settlement.
title: League of Nations comes into force
description: The new international organization begins work under the postwar settlement.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Atlantic Charter proclaims self-determination principles
description: The wartime charter helps frame later anti-colonial claims, even if unevenly applied.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: United Nations is founded
description: The UN becomes a central international forum for self-determination and decolonization.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Philippines gains independence from the United States
description: The Republic of the Philippines becomes formally independent.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: India and Pakistan become independent
description: The end of British rule in South Asia creates two dominions amid partition.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Burma becomes independent
description: Burma leaves British rule and establishes an independent state.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Dutch transfer sovereignty to Indonesia
description: The Netherlands formally recognizes Indonesian independence after armed struggle and diplomacy.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Libya becomes independent
description: Libya becomes the first country to achieve independence through the United Nations process.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: French defeat at Dien Bien Phu
description: The collapse of French power in Indochina accelerates decolonization in Southeast Asia.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Morocco becomes independent
description: France recognizes Moroccan independence, ending the protectorate.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Tunisia becomes independent
description: Tunisia ends French protectorate rule and becomes sovereign.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Ghana becomes independent
description: Ghana becomes the first sub-Saharan African colony to gain independence from British rule in the postwar era.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: UN adopts the Declaration on the Granting of Independence to Colonial Countries and Peoples
description: General Assembly Resolution 1514 becomes a foundational anti-colonial declaration.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Year of Africa transforms the continent
description: Seventeen African countries achieve independence in a single year.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Special Committee on Decolonization is established
description: The UN creates the C-24 to monitor implementation of decolonization commitments.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Algeria becomes independent
description: Algerian independence ends a long and violent war against French rule.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Kenya becomes independent
description: Kenya leaves British rule and becomes a sovereign state.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Carnation Revolution begins Portuguese decolonization
description: The Portuguese dictatorship falls, opening rapid independence for African colonies.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Mozambique becomes independent
description: Mozambique ends Portuguese colonial rule.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Angola becomes independent
description: Angola leaves Portuguese rule but enters a prolonged civil war.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Zimbabwe becomes independent
description: Majority rule is established in the former Rhodesia.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Namibia becomes independent
description: Namibia gains internationally recognized independence after long contestation.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: Hong Kong is handed over to China
description: British colonial rule in Hong Kong ends with the transfer of sovereignty to China.

timeline_description: Major global milestones in the end of colonial rule from the Second World War era to late twentieth-century transfers of sovereignty.
title: East Timor becomes independent
description: Timor-Leste becomes a sovereign state after UN administration and earlier Indonesian occupation.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Fourier describes the greenhouse effect
description: Joseph Fourier explains how the atmosphere can trap heat and warm the planet.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Eunice Foote demonstrates the warming effect of carbon dioxide
description: Foote's experiments show that carbon dioxide can heat enclosed air more strongly than ordinary air.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Tyndall identifies heat-trapping gases
description: John Tyndall experimentally shows that certain gases absorb radiant heat.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Arrhenius calculates the climatic effect of carbon dioxide
description: Svante Arrhenius estimates that changes in atmospheric carbon dioxide could alter global temperature.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Callendar links rising carbon dioxide to warming
description: Guy Stewart Callendar argues that observed temperature rise may be connected to fossil fuel emissions.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Keeling begins continuous carbon dioxide measurements at Mauna Loa
description: The Keeling Curve becomes a foundational record of atmospheric carbon dioxide increase.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: U.S. presidential science advisers warn about climate effects of carbon dioxide
description: A federal scientific report highlights the potential climatic consequences of rising emissions.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Stockholm Conference elevates environmental issues globally
description: The UN conference helps build an international framework for environmental science and policy.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Charney Report estimates likely warming from doubled carbon dioxide
description: A U.S. scientific assessment provides a foundational range for climate sensitivity.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: First World Climate Conference is held
description: Scientists and governments call for coordinated international climate research.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Villach Conference warns of significant warming
description: An influential scientific meeting strengthens the case for coordinated international action.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC is established
description: UNEP and WMO create the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC releases its First Assessment Report
description: The first major assessment synthesizes scientific understanding of climate change.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: UNFCCC is adopted
description: Governments establish the main international framework convention on climate change.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Kyoto Protocol is adopted
description: The protocol creates binding emissions targets for many industrialized countries.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC Third Assessment Report is published
description: The assessment strengthens scientific attribution of observed warming.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC Fourth Assessment Report is published
description: The report states that warming of the climate system is unequivocal and receives major global attention.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: Paris Agreement is adopted
description: The agreement sets a global framework for limiting warming and strengthening climate action.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC Special Report on Global Warming of 1.5C is released
description: The report clarifies the consequences of 1.5C and 2C warming and the scale of mitigation needed.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC Working Group I contribution to the Sixth Assessment Report is released
description: The report concludes that human influence has unequivocally warmed the atmosphere, ocean, and land.

timeline_description: Major scientific discoveries, assessments, and international milestones in the development of climate change science.
title: IPCC Sixth Assessment Synthesis Report is released
description: The synthesis report integrates the latest assessment cycle and informs the global stocktake era.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Abacus is in use
description: The abacus becomes the earliest widely known calculating device in computing history.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Pascal invents the Pascaline
description: Blaise Pascal builds a mechanical calculator for arithmetic operations.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Leibniz demonstrates a stepped reckoner
description: Leibniz advances mechanical calculation with multiplication and division capability.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Jacquard loom uses punched cards
description: The loom introduces programmable control through punched cards.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Babbage proposes the Difference Engine
description: Charles Babbage designs a machine intended to automate table calculation.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Ada Lovelace publishes notes on the Analytical Engine
description: Her notes include a celebrated algorithm and a broad vision of machine computation.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Hollerith tabulators are used in the U.S. census
description: Punch-card tabulation dramatically speeds large-scale data processing.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Turing describes a universal computing machine
description: Alan Turing's paper provides a foundational theoretical model of computation.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Zuse completes the Z3
description: The Z3 becomes an important early programmable digital computer.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Colossus project begins
description: British codebreaking work produces one of the first electronic digital computing systems.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: ENIAC is unveiled
description: ENIAC becomes the first programmable general-purpose electronic digital computer to gain wide recognition.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Transistor is invented
description: The transistor lays the foundation for modern electronic computing.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: UNIVAC I enters service
description: UNIVAC I becomes the first commercial computer delivered in the United States.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Integrated circuit is invented
description: Integrated circuits make smaller and more powerful computers possible.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: ARPANET sends its first message
description: Packet-switched networking creates a foundational step toward the internet age.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Intel introduces the 4004 microprocessor
description: The microprocessor brings central processing onto a single chip.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Altair 8800 popularizes personal computing
description: The Altair becomes a landmark early personal computer kit.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: IBM PC is released
description: IBM's personal computer helps standardize the PC market.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Berners-Lee proposes the World Wide Web
description: The proposal outlines a hypertext system that transforms networked information access.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: Linux kernel is announced
description: Linux becomes a major open-source operating system foundation.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the history of computing from early calculating devices to networked and personal computing.
title: iPhone launches the smartphone era in mass computing
description: The iPhone accelerates the shift toward mobile, touch-based personal computing.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Thirteenth Amendment abolishes slavery
description: The amendment ends slavery and involuntary servitude except as punishment for crime.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Fourteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment establishes birthright citizenship and equal protection under the law.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Fifteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment prohibits denial of the vote based on race, color, or previous condition of servitude.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Plessy v. Ferguson upholds segregation
description: The Supreme Court legitimizes the doctrine of separate but equal.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: NAACP is founded
description: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People begins national civil rights advocacy.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Executive Order 8802 bans discrimination in defense industries
description: Federal policy begins addressing employment discrimination during World War II mobilization.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Truman orders desegregation of the armed forces
description: Executive Order 9981 commits the military to equal treatment and opportunity.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Brown v. Board of Education is decided
description: The Supreme Court rules that segregated public schools are inherently unequal.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Montgomery bus boycott begins
description: The boycott becomes a major victory for nonviolent mass protest.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Little Rock Nine integrate Central High School
description: Federal intervention enforces school desegregation in Arkansas.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Greensboro sit-ins begin
description: Student sit-ins spread across the South and energize direct-action protest.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Freedom Rides challenge segregated interstate travel
description: Integrated activists test enforcement of desegregation rulings and face mob violence.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Birmingham campaign confronts segregation
description: Mass protest and official repression in Birmingham reshape national opinion.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: March on Washington is held
description: The march dramatizes demands for jobs, freedom, and civil rights legislation.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Civil Rights Act is signed
description: The act outlaws discrimination in public accommodations, employment, and federally funded programs.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Selma to Montgomery marches focus national attention on voting rights
description: The marches expose violent resistance to Black enfranchisement in Alabama.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Voting Rights Act is signed
description: The law becomes a central federal safeguard for Black voting rights.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Loving v. Virginia strikes down bans on interracial marriage
description: The Supreme Court invalidates laws prohibiting interracial marriage.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Fair Housing Act is enacted
description: The act prohibits discrimination in the sale, rental, and financing of housing.
source_publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Regents of the University of California v. Bakke is decided
description: The Supreme Court permits some affirmative action while rejecting rigid racial quotas.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Americans with Disabilities Act is signed
description: The ADA extends civil rights protections to people with disabilities.

timeline_title: Timeline of Civil Rights in the United States
timeline_description: Major legal, political, and grassroots milestones in the struggle for civil rights in the United States.
title: Civil Rights Act of 1991 is enacted
description: The act strengthens federal remedies in employment discrimination cases.
